work_package_result.summary: Added bounded privacy-safe local diagnostics for Dennett Node, Head and the Codex adapter host: structured evidence survives restart without prompts, responses, credentials, paths or hidden reasoning; lifecycle records distinguish clean, handled and unclean exits; the Node data root and SQLite path are guarded against link and replacement races; and dennettctl doctor reports an honest local health summary.

passed: 41 dennett-observability tests cover persistence, rotation, redaction, non-blocking disk and console behavior, exact drop accounting, lifecycle recovery, clock rollback, corrupt records, sequence exhaustion, bounded publication and startup rollback

15 Node unit and runtime-host tests cover trusted roots, preplanted database and sidecar links, profile locking, bounded framing and queues, control timeouts, unknown responses, stderr classification and host fencing
5 detached-process diagnostic tests cover clean, handled and unclean restart evidence, unavailable storage fallback and safe adapter-host failure classification
61 adapter-host tests passed, including subscription authentication, Codex discovery, streaming, cancellation, timeout, continuation, native steering and bounded process behavior
3 dennettctl doctor tests cover explicit profile selection, typed human-readable output and JSON output that excludes local paths
mise exec -- uv run --project . --frozen python tools/run_in_toolchain.py cargo test -p dennett-observability -p dennett-node -p dennett-head passed on the merge-ready implementation
mise exec -- corepack pnpm --filter @dennett/adapter-host-node test passed all 61 tests
mise exec -- just check passed repository, documentation, planning, CI, format, clippy, workspace tests, type checks and protocol checks

the Linux dennett-observability target compiled with warnings denied; a separate Linux SQLite probe confirmed that /proc/self/fd keeps database writes bound to the opened directory after display-path replacement
2 ignored live subscription tests were run explicitly after the final lifecycle repair and passed: same-turn Codex steering and continuation of the same Codex session after a real Node restart
not_run: long-duration soak, fuzzing, disk-full endurance and resource-exhaustion campaigns remain release-wide hardening
full native Node execution on Linux awaits Ubuntu CI because this Windows workstation has no Linux C cross-compiler for bundled SQLite
provider adapters other than Codex and local models remain unavailable by owner constraint and are represented by provider-neutral ports plus deterministic fakes

review.method: Successive detached read-only R2 audits reviewed privacy, filesystem authority, runtime-host recovery and lifecycle ordering against exact commits. Every confirmed P2 finding received a concrete regression and a fresh review. Final review of exact diff 27deef23551991bd686736339dbbbb99e74c95d8..dc29aa14e5fcc48c90fc88c60f63a29d2b3c7b9f returned no P0-P2 findings after the full gate and live Codex canaries passed.
findings_closed: made persistent logging and console fallback bounded and non-blocking, with explicit queue, storage and flush-loss evidence
made clean, failed, unclean and unknown lifecycle states durable without claiming unavailable operating-system or provider causes
removed prompt, response, credential, hidden-reasoning, raw provider error and absolute local-path leakage from durable diagnostics and doctor JSON
fenced runtime-host deadlocks, unknown responses, blocked writes, overflowing queues and late generations with typed safe terminal evidence
opened every profile path component no-follow, rejected linked SQLite files and sidecars, avoided mutating system-ancestor ACLs and bound Linux SQLite paths to the opened directory capability
reserved canonically observed corrupt run numbers before cleanup and persisted a monotonic high-water marker across startup rollback and integer exhaustion
replaced uniquely accumulating lifecycle temp files with one per-component locked temp name so repeated interrupted writes remain bounded

migrations: introduced disposable private lifecycle schema version 2 and bounded local diagnostic files under the profile diagnostics directory; no canonical user data or project history migration is required
observability_changes: privacy-safe run, project, session, command and runtime-turn UUID correlations survive process restart without content
lifecycle checkpoints retain the last durable phase, drop count completeness, flush status, clock anomaly and clean/failed/unclean outcome
dennettctl doctor exposes bounded log volume, active or stale runs, unreadable evidence and the latest trustworthy terminal state in text or path-free JSON
Node startup and adapter-host failures emit fixed diagnostic codes while unavailable file diagnostics degrade to bounded console output
known_limitations: M02 currently exposes diagnostics through dennettctl doctor; a clickable desktop Diagnostics workspace and support-bundle export remain later work
when the operating system or Codex supplies no precise cause, diagnostics intentionally report the last durable phase and a generic safe classification
Windows and Linux have stable guarded SQLite paths; other native platforms fail closed until their storage adapter gains an equivalent capability-relative open

the real subscription canaries are an explicit closure gate rather than generic CI because CI does not own the user's ChatGPT authentication and cannot distinguish provider outage or quota from a product regression
follow_up_candidates: WP-M02-002 stable project identity and relocatable WorkspaceBinding after owner resolution of DEC-0006
desktop Diagnostics workspace and privacy-reviewed support-bundle export
release-wide persistence soak, fuzzing, disk-full and resource-exhaustion qualification
additional provider and local-model conformance suites when access becomes available
